title: Labor Force Participation Rate by Gender x Urban/Rural, SCI/MCLS (2005-2014) -- content-duplicate copy
primary_source: iran-disasters-regional-series [DUPLICATE -- no new data, see notes]
notes: FOLLOW-UP to registration_completeness_audit.csv's dedup note on the iran_data_portal__labor_force_participation_rate_urbanrural_2005_2014 row. Verified via a full row-by-row diff (all 90 rows: 10 years x 3 areas x 3 sexes, Iranian year 1384-1393 / gregorian 2005-2014) that data/processed/iran_disasters_regional_series/labor_force_participation_rate_2005_2014.csv is BYTE-IDENTICAL in every value to the already-registered data/processed/iran_data_portal_deep_series/labor_force_participation_rate_gender_urbanrural_2005_2014.csv (same SCI/Ministry of Cooperatives Labour and Social Welfare source, e.g. 41%/64.7%/17% national/men/women 2005-06 matches exactly) -- only the column labels differ (area='total' vs 'national', sex='Both'/'Men'/'Women' vs gender='both_genders'/'men'/'women') plus 3 added metadata columns (unit, source, country_iso3) carrying no new information. CORRECTION to the audit's earlier guess that this file might be 'missing 2014-2015': it is not -- both files cover the exact same span through Iranian year 1393 (2014-2015 gregorian), confirmed identical, no coverage gap either direction. Registered here as a DEDUP ANNOTATION rather than a genuine alt-source: this row exists so the file is no longer un-registered (closes the last known orphan from the audit), but should NOT be read as independent corroboration -- it is the same underlying SCI/MCLS numbers reformatted, not a second data collection. If this file is ever pruned as pure redundancy, no information is lost; the identical figures remain in iran_data_portal_deep_series.
